extra_model_fields.estimated_effort: Small (update workbook and grower_plan.md, generate CSV/JSON summary, run parse check).
extra_model_fields.expected_benefit: Improved machine-verifiability and grower usability; reduces verifier friction and prevents preview/parsing failures from blocking independent checks.
extra_model_fields.primary_verifier_concern_addressed: xlsx_preview_unavailable (add README, maxima cells, and a parseable summary)

extra_model_fields.estimated_effort: Small (update workbook and grower_plan.md, generate CSV/JSON summary, run parse check).

extra_model_fields.primary_verifier_concern_addressed: xlsx_preview_unavailable (add README, maxima cells, and a parseable summary)

planned_changes: Add a top-level README sheet in irrigation_deficit_optimizer.xlsx listing sheet names, the purpose of each sheet, and exact cell ranges used for inputs, maxima, normalized values, weighted scores, and final outputs.

Create explicit maxima cells (one per metric: crop_value_usd_ha, water_mm, yield_response_pct, soil_moisture_pct) and reference those cells in normalization formulas instead of implicit max calculations embedded in formulas.

Add a clearly labeled input cell for unit-cost (e.g., USD per mm per ha or USD per mm for the affected area) with instructions and wire it to compute estimated expenditure for any proposed mm reduction.
Add a budget-expenditure check cell that compares estimated expenditure (when unit-cost is provided) to decision_budget_usd and flags overspend.
Document an explicit tie-breaker rule both in grower_plan.md and as a note in the workbook (e.g., prefer higher crop value, or stable random seed) to remove ambiguity when scores tie.

Expose multi-record allocation logic stepwise in the 'Optimization' sheet (if applicable): show iterative allocation steps or provide a small worked example and formula cells so verifiers can reproduce re-distribution logic.

Produce a parseable summary file (CSV and/or JSON) that lists for each record: raw inputs, maxima used, normalized values, weighted score, rank, classification, and recommended water cut. Include this alongside the .xlsx deliverable.

Confirm workbook save settings ensure compatibility with openpyxl and, if possible, run a quick parse test in CI to validate that the .xlsx is parseable; if environment constraints remain, include the parseable summary as primary machine-checkable evidence.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Address minor verifier limitation (xlsx preview/parsing unavailable) and improve workbook transparency and grower-facing completeness (README, maxima cells, unit-cost temporary stand-in, budget check, tie-breaker rule).

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline attempt meets the rubric and received full credit. Calculations, ranking, classification, sensitivity check, and recommendation (cap R2 to 30.35 mm; reduce 26.80 mm) are consistent across the grower plan, actual outputs, and trace. The only verification limitation was that the .xlsx preview could not be parsed by the verifier (openpyxl unavailable), so independent, machine-checkable formula inspection was not possible. Cost conversion was intentionally left as 'needs_review' because the packet omitted unit-cost inputs.
